zeroComponentIdsInLowererControlFlow.evidence: grep across lowering/{vue,svelte,solid}/lower.mjs and lowering/core/core.mjs found NO component-name branches (button/checkbox/field/popover). Only matches are semantic-role lookups (semantics==='checkbox' -> input type, semantics==='button' -> tag), which are role-driven, not component-identity-driven.
zeroComponentIdsInLowererControlFlow.note: This is the single most important positive architectural result: lowerers are genuinely generic.

compileSpeed.note: Pure compile/lowering is comfortably sub-second. Browser proof (build+SSR+hydrate+CDP capture of 36 cells) is the dominant cost, ~minutes per target, identical cost burden for hand-port lane.

currentParity.rootCause: Canonical React click logs after.events=[{type:'click'}]; native captures show after.events=[]. The generated event-listen dispatch did not produce a logged raw event during the trusted CDP click. Vue/Solid populate behavior.action; Svelte behavior.action was empty ({}) — separate target-detection bug in svelte capture. This is a wiring gap, not a structural impossibility.

visualOnlyPeak.note: Before behavior strictness, full-matrix peaked at 48/108 exact (Vue 30/36 incl. Checkbox 9/9 + Field 9/9 exact, Svelte 18/36, Solid valid captures). Visual parity for primitives/forms is demonstrably reachable.
